# Helicon Works

> AI consulting from Rohit Sharma: production AI systems — agents, automation, evals, guardrails, governance — for companies that want AI doing real work, not stalled pilots. Operator background: co-founder of Kanmon (embedded lending, $23M+ raised from Bessemer, Homebrew, Inspired, FIS), ex-VP Product & Design at Funding Circle (56x growth, $9.5B+ originated, LSE IPO), GM at Plastiq, Director of PM at Dun & Bradstreet.

## Positioning

Most AI consulting ends in a deck. This practice ends in production. The thesis: the model isn't the bottleneck — the harness is (the tool layer, evals, guardrails, and governance that let AI do real work safely).

## Offers

- AI Opportunity Audit — 2–3 weeks, fixed fee: ranked roadmap of AI wins plus a working prototype of the #1 item
- Agentic Engineering Transformation — 4–8 weeks embedded with engineering orgs: coding-agent rollout, MCP tool layer over internal systems, AI-native SDLC, measured on cycle time
- Ops Automation Build — 4–8 weeks for finance/ops/compliance: one production workflow, or a full embedded "AI employee" that learns the business and takes over jobs — with permissions, evals, and an audit trail
- Fractional AI Lead — monthly retainer: own the AI roadmap, review builds, vendor/model decisions, exec reporting

## Proof (qualitative)

- Autonomous underwriting: one command produces a bank-grade credit memo with non-waivable credit rules applied identically to every file
- Daily portfolio risk monitor: agent-prototyped signals, deterministic SQL in production, watchlist in Slack before the team logs on
- Company-wide Slack agent that builds its own skill library (RFP analysis: an afternoon → minutes)
- Customer-facing agent and document parsing in a lending transaction path, with evals, model fallbacks, and circuit breakers
- losaltos.space — a self-maintaining neighborhood directory built end to end with agents in four days
